Sent: Wednesday, November 13, 2019 12:12:24 PM
Subject: IMPORTANT Message from the Office of Academic Links, CUHK (13 November 2019) #5

Dear students,

We would like to provide you with an update on the social unrest in Hong Kong. Hopefully this will give you a good perspective of the situation and clarification on some of the misleading information presented by the media.

Yesterday (12 November), there were violent confrontations between protestors and police at Bridge 2 near the east side entrance of the CUHK campus. At one point in the afternoon, the confrontation advanced to the Haddon-Cave Sports Field (opposite Bridge 2) before retreating to Bridge 2. Confrontation did not progress into the campus beyond the sports field and police did not enter any of the residential halls. The stand-off lasted until around mid-night before both sides backed down with no further clashes. Attached is a map showing where confrontations occurred so to give you a perspective of the location in respect to the campus. Throughout the day, alerts were posted on our closed Facebook Group to remind and urge you to avoid areas where confrontations were taking place.

Classes continue to be cancelled today (13 November). The three University entrances are currently inaccessible by vehicles but accessible on foot. Several canteens, as well as the supermarket, are operating for some parts of the day. Moving forward, our office will continue to monitor the situation very closely and update you through social media as well as email on the situation at CUHK. Our 24/7 emergency hotline continue to operate to support any of you needing urgent assistance.

We understand that some of you are worried about personal safety and are considering leaving CUHK early due to the continued unrest in Hong Kong. We fully respect your decisions and will provide all the support available for a smooth transition home. For those who decide to return home early, we urge you to get in touch with us and we will liaise with your course teachers and departments to facilitate continuation of your courses remotely from your home countries. A simple email to tell us when you are leaving will be sufficient and we will take care of the administrative procedures. For those who intend to stay for the remainder of the semester, you can also let us know so we can continue to provide support and assistance when necessary.

If you have any questions, please reach out to your Advisor via email or their emergency numbers below.
（保障私隱，不公開其國際生的原大學 聯絡資訊）
Best regards,
（保障私隱，不公開其國際生的就讀課程）
Office of Academic Links"

-------------------------------------------------------
Wrong Chinese translation

We understand that some of you are worried about personal safety and are considering leaving CUHK early due to the continued unrest in Hong Kong. We fully respect your decisions and will provide all the support available for a smooth transition home. For those who decide to return home early, we urge you to get in touch with us and we will liaise with your course teachers and departments to facilitate continuation of your courses remotely from your home countries. A simple email to tell us when you are leaving will be sufficient and we will take care of the administrative procedures. For those who intend to stay for the remainder of the semester, you can also let us know so we can continue to provide support and assistance when necessary.
